title: Determine the exact value of R(B11, B11)
micro_paper_assessment: Still not closed enough for the micro-paper lane, but now there is a genuine slice-level packet: any 45-vertex obstruction must be a non-Cayley srg(45,22,10,11).
family_affinity: high; the n=11 diagonal residue collapses to a conference-graph obstruction class, with all regular Cayley families on 45 vertices eliminated
generalization_signal: moderate; the Goodman-tightness reduction may transfer to other one-gap diagonal book residues, though the non-Cayley existence question is specific to n=11
candidate_theorem_slice: Any 45-vertex graph whose graph and complement are both B11-free must be strongly regular with parameters (45,22,10,11); no symmetric Cayley graph on a group of order 45 has these parameters.
next_action: If solve continues, it should target non-Cayley conference-graph structure or a nonexistence argument for srg(45,22,10,11), not more abelian regular-action search.
